diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper GI haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Melaena is digested blood and is near-specific for a significant upper GI source of bleeding.
Lower GI bleeds usually present as bright-red haematochezia, not melaena.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic burning/gnawing epigastric pain relieved by food and antacids, worse at night
symptoms.character: burning/gnawing, between meals and early morning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Nocturnal pain relieved by food is the classic duodenal ulcer pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Presyncope / lightheadedness and weakness on standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemia / haemorrhagic shock from GI bleed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural symptoms indicate significant intravascular volume loss from ongoing haemorrhage.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pallor and diaphoresis (looks pale and sweaty)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemia / haemorrhagic shock from GI bleed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral vasoconstriction and sympathetic response to acute blood loss; the caregiver can observe pallor/sweating, though conjunctival pallor requires examination.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper GI haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: NSAIDs are a leading cause of peptic ulceration and ulcer bleeding, especially without a PPI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden change to severe, constant, unrelieved epigastric pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Acute complication of peptic ulcer (bleeding; exclude perforation)
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A change in the character of chronic pain flags an acute complication requiring urgent assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper GI haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of haematemesis does not exclude an upper GI bleed presenting as melaena; the bleed can be distal or slow.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unintentional weight loss or dysphagia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of weight loss/dysphagia makes malignancy less likely but does NOT exclude it — endoscopy still required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemia / haemorrhagic shock from GI bleed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective postural blood-pressure drop confirms significant volume depletion — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI infers this from reported presyncope, not a measured value.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypovolaemia / haemorrhagic shock from GI bleed
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia for blood loss; not measurable in telehealth without a device.

symptoms.symptom_name: Melaena confirmed on digital rectal examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper GI haemorrhage
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective clinician confirmation of melaena; a DRE cannot be performed in telehealth — sealed answer key.

symptoms.symptom_name: Anaemia on full blood count (haemoglobin drop)

investigation_details.expected_result: Low or falling haemoglobin consistent with acute blood loss (exact value not available remotely)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Degree of blood loss and transfusion need remain unquantified.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies blood loss and guides transfusion; a hospital investigation, never available in real time via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive urea breath test for Helicobacter pylori

investigation_details.expected_result: Positive (presumed) — confirming H. pylori as an underlying driver
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Underlying cause not confirmed; recurrence risk if not eradicated (performed after acute stabilisation, not in the emergency).

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Establishes H. pylori as a treatable underlying cause; a deferred outpatient/inpatient test, not part of the acute telehealth decision.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute upper GI bleed with haemodynamic compromise
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Bleeding peptic ulcer / upper GI haemorrhage with hypovolaemic shock
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Melaena plus orthostatic symptoms, pallor and tachycardia together define an emergency haemorrhage — the constellation that must trigger 000.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: NSAID- and H. pylori-related peptic ulcer disease
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Explains the underlying lesion that has now bled; drives the cause-directed management (stop NSAID, eradicate H. pylori, PPI).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency ambulance transfer and hospital resuscitation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Haemodynamic compromise from active bleeding requires immediate IV access, fluid/blood resuscitation and monitoring.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count, urea/creatinine, coagulation, group and hold / crossmatch
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies blood loss, assesses coagulopathy and prepares for transfusion.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent upper GI endoscopy (within 24 hours, after resuscitation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Identifies and treats the bleeding lesion (endoscopic haemostasis) and excludes malignancy.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urea breath test / H. pylori testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and directs eradication of the underlying cause — performed after acute stabilisation, not in the emergency.
Melaena indicates an upper source; upper endoscopy is the correct first investigation, not colonoscopy.
